summary: Package's sole export reads root-owned flag files and enumerates processes
details: The package exports a single function `command()` whose body shells out via `execSync` to read `/tmp/root_flag.txt` and `/root/root.txt` and to run `ps aux | grep local_check`, printing the output to stdout. There are no install or import-time side effects (no lifecycle scripts, no top-level execution), and the behavior only fires when a consumer explicitly invokes `require('internallib_v557').command()`. The hardcoded paths and the `local_check` marker indicate this is CTF/red-team reconnaissance scaffolding rather than a real library. There is no network exfiltration channel and no install-time destruction, so this does not satisfy the supply-chain attack threshold, but the package provides no legitimate functionality and its only purpose is to probe sensitive host files when called.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
